title: Understanding the Problem
text: To find the ratio of the centripetal force to the child's weight, we need to calculate both forces and then divide the centripetal force by the weight.

title: Calculate the Centripetal Force
text: The centripetal force can be calculated using the formula: $$F_c=\frac{m v^2}{r}$$, where $$m$$ is the mass, v is the velocity, and $$r$$ is the radius. First, convert the rotational speed from revolutions per minute to meters per second.

title: Calculate the Weight
text: The weight of the child can be calculated using the formula: $$W=m g$$, where $$m$$ is the mass and g is the acceleration due to gravity $$(9.8$$ m/s²).

title: Find the Ratio
text: Now that you have both the centripetal force and the weight, find their ratio by dividing the centripetal force by the weight.
